title: rubus scaber var. meyeri
intro: Rubus scaber var. meyeri, also known as the Japanese thimbleberry, is a variety of thorny shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus scaber var. meyeri include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Rubus scaber var. meyeri can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
